title: Commercial Mortgages in Greater Vancouver
metaTitle: Commercial Mortgage in BC — Multi-Family, Retail, Mixed-Use
metaDescription: Commercial mortgages in Greater Vancouver for multi-family rentals, retail, mixed-use, office and industrial properties. DSCR-based qualifying and lender programs.
intro: Commercial mortgages run on different math — Debt Service Coverage Ratio, not GDS/TDS. The property's cash flow does most of the qualifying; your personal income is supporting evidence.

body.heading: How commercial qualifying works
paragraphs: The key metric is DSCR — Debt Service Coverage Ratio — calculated as (Net Operating Income) ÷ (Annual Debt Service). Most commercial lenders want a DSCR of 1.20 or higher, meaning the property's income covers 120% of mortgage payments. Strong files at well-located properties can push DSCR requirements down to 1.10; weaker properties need 1.30+.

Loan-to-value caps are lower than residential: 65–75% on most commercial property types. Multi-family rentals (5+ units) sit in a separate program category — CMHC and Sagen have insured multi-family programs allowing higher LTV (up to 85%) at preferential rates.

body.heading: Property types and their nuances
paragraphs: Multi-family (5+ units): the friendliest commercial category. CMHC insured programs offer 5- to 10-year terms at sub-residential rates. Retail and mixed-use: pricing depends on tenant mix, lease structure and location. Office: post-2020 risk premium remains; lenders favour single-tenant, long-lease properties. Industrial: very lender-friendly currently due to limited supply in Greater Vancouver.
Personal guarantees are typically required, even for corporate-held commercial mortgages. Sophisticated borrowers can negotiate limited or burn-off guarantees as their portfolio matures.

whoItsFor: Are buying a 5+ unit multi-family rental in Greater Vancouver
Are buying a mixed-use, retail, office or industrial property
Are refinancing a commercial property to release equity or improve terms

faqs.question: What rates do commercial mortgages get?
faqs.answer: Multi-family with CMHC insurance: often 0.5–1% below residential rates. Conventional commercial: 0.5–1.5% above residential. Specialty asset classes (hospitality, gas stations): higher again.

faqs.question: Do I need a corporation to hold commercial property?
faqs.answer: Most commercial deals are corp-held for tax and liability reasons, but personal-held is possible. Talk to your accountant about the right structure for your specific portfolio and tax picture.

faqs.question: How long do commercial deals take?
faqs.answer: Typically 60–120 days from accepted offer to funding, including appraisal, environmental, title and underwriting. Plan timelines accordingly.
